title: Tabgha Base
wikitext:
[[Image:Eva2-22 C0300 comp.jpg|left|thumb|200px|Blood streak and Tabgha Base]]
[[Image:Eva1-01_C1680_moon-giant.jpg|right|thumb|200px|[[Kaworu]] and the "Moon Giant"]]
[[Image:Eva2-22 C0303 crop-edit.jpg|right|thumb|200px|[[Evangelion Unit-06|Evangelion Mark.06]] under construction at Tabgha Base]]
[[Image:Eva2-22 C0306 comp.jpg|right|thumb|200px|Mark.06's [[Spear of Cassius|Spear]] in wrappings]]
'''Tabgha Base''' is the 7th Branch of [[Nerv]], stationed in Mare Tranquilitatis on the [[Moon]].<ref name=exp1>[[Resources:Explanation of Evangelion 1.01 (Pt. 2)|''Explanation of Evangelion 1.01'']]</ref>

The base is situated on one edge of the giant red "blood stain". In [[Evangelion 1.0]], the base has a highly unfinished appearance, and is referred to as a "Provisional Excavation Base"<ref name=exp1/> although precisely what was being excavated has not been revealed.

Tabgha Base was responsible for the construction of [[Evangelion Unit-06|Evangelion Mark.06]]. [[Kaworu Nagisa]] was also stationed here until his deployment at the end of [[Evangelion 2.0]]. Mark.06's [[Spear of Cassius|Spear]] is shown in wrappings in the process of relocation when Gendo and Fuyutsuki visit the base.

==Coffins==
At the end of Evangelion 1.0, Kaworu emerges from one of nine visible coffin-like objects placed in front of Eva-06, with four to one side of him having already been opened, and the other four still closed. The ending of [[Evangelion 3.0+1.0]] reveals that there are far more than nine coffins and they stretch out in a large circle. Going by Kaworu's dialogue about a "predestined circular narrative for all of eternity", the coffins may actually be innumerable in the sense that the looping of the narrative will not cease when the final one opens; perhaps they will reset and begin opening again.

==Allusions==
*Real-life Tabgha (from Heptapagon, "''seven'' springs") is a valley near the Sea of Galilee in northern Israel. (Tabgha Base is the ''seven''th branch of Nerv.) It is considered to be where Jesus Christ reassembled the disciples, and where Peter was reinstated after denying Jesus three times.<ref>[http://www.churchisraelforum.com/Tabgha_the_old_fishing_hole.htm Church & Israfel Forum. Tabgha - The Old Fishing Hole.] Retrieved 2011-02-27.</ref> The miracle of multiplication performed by Jesus Christ in the New Testament, whereby he fed 5000 people with five loaves of bread and two fish, occurred here according to some traditions.<ref>[http://www.welcometohosanna.com/LIFE_OF_JESUS/025_Ministry5Tabgha.htm Jesus' Life and Times. Tabgha.] Retrieved 2011-02-27.</ref>

* In one of many references to Stanley Kubrick's film 2001: A Space Odyssey, the pit in which Eva-06 is being excavated and/or constructed looks very similar to the TMA-1 monolith excavation site on the moon. Similarly, the spacecraft used by Gendo and Fuyutsuki to spy on the proceedings draws design elements from both the lunar shuttle and moon rocket bus, and the denial of landing rights to their spacecraft references a conversation between Dr. Floyd and Russian scientists during the same movie.

|[[Image:Kaworu coffins in 3.0+1.0 1.jpg|400px|center]]
|[[Image:Kaworu coffins in 3.0+1.0 2.jpg|400px|center]]
|}

html:
Tabgha Base is the 7th Branch of Nerv, stationed in Mare Tranquilitatis on the Moon.[1]

The base is situated on one edge of the giant red "blood stain". In Evangelion 1.0, the base has a highly unfinished appearance, and is referred to as a "Provisional Excavation Base"[1] although precisely what was being excavated has not been revealed.

Tabgha Base was responsible for the construction of Evangelion Mark.06. Kaworu Nagisa was also stationed here until his deployment at the end of Evangelion 2.0. Mark.06's Spear is shown in wrappings in the process of relocation when Gendo and Fuyutsuki visit the base.

Coffins[edit]

At the end of Evangelion 1.0, Kaworu emerges from one of nine visible coffin-like objects placed in front of Eva-06, with four to one side of him having already been opened, and the other four still closed. The ending of Evangelion 3.0+1.0 reveals that there are far more than nine coffins and they stretch out in a large circle. Going by Kaworu's dialogue about a "predestined circular narrative for all of eternity", the coffins may actually be innumerable in the sense that the looping of the narrative will not cease when the final one opens; perhaps they will reset and begin opening again.

Allusions[edit]

  • Real-life Tabgha (from Heptapagon, "seven springs") is a valley near the Sea of Galilee in northern Israel. (Tabgha Base is the seventh branch of Nerv.) It is considered to be where Jesus Christ reassembled the disciples, and where Peter was reinstated after denying Jesus three times.[2] The miracle of multiplication performed by Jesus Christ in the New Testament, whereby he fed 5000 people with five loaves of bread and two fish, occurred here according to some traditions.[3]
  • In one of many references to Stanley Kubrick's film 2001: A Space Odyssey, the pit in which Eva-06 is being excavated and/or constructed looks very similar to the TMA-1 monolith excavation site on the moon. Similarly, the spacecraft used by Gendo and Fuyutsuki to spy on the proceedings draws design elements from both the lunar shuttle and moon rocket bus, and the denial of landing rights to their spacecraft references a conversation between Dr. Floyd and Russian scientists during the same movie.

References[edit]

  1. ↑ 1.0 1.1 Explanation of Evangelion 1.01
  2. ↑ Church & Israfel Forum. Tabgha - The Old Fishing Hole. Retrieved 2011-02-27.
  3. ↑ Jesus' Life and Times. Tabgha. Retrieved 2011-02-27.
